questions.q: In which town is the Chennakeshava Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Chennakeshava Temple stands in the town of Belur in Karnataka.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Chennakeshava Temple at Belur situated?
questions.explanation: Belur is in the Hassan district of the southern state of Karnataka.

questions.q: Which dynasty built the Chennakeshava Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is a celebrated example of Hoysala architecture, built under Hoysala rule.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Chennakeshava Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: Chennakeshava is a form of Vishnu, so the temple is a Vaishnava shrine.

questions.q: In which century was the Chennakeshava Temple at Belur built?
questions.explanation: Construction was commissioned in the 12th century during the Hoysala era.

questions.q: Which Hoysala king is credited with commissioning the Chennakeshava Temple?
questions.explanation: King Vishnuvardhana of the Hoysala dynasty commissioned the temple.

questions.q: The name 'Chennakeshava' is most accurately translated as which of the following?
questions.explanation: 'Chenna' means beautiful or handsome, and Keshava is a name of Vishnu.

questions.q: The Chennakeshava Temple is most renowned for which artistic feature?
questions.explanation: The temple is famous for its richly detailed sculpted bracket figures and ornamentation.

questions.q: What is the subtitle nickname commonly used for the Chennakeshava Temple in this deck?
questions.explanation: The temple is celebrated as 'The Belur Jewel' for its exquisite craftsmanship.

questions.q: Belur, the site of the Chennakeshava Temple, served as an important center for which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Belur was an early Hoysala capital and a major Hoysala center.

questions.q: The Chennakeshava Temple is widely said to have been built to commemorate which event?
options: The founding of a new river port
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the temple commemorated Vishnuvardhana's victory, often linked to his triumph over the Cholas at Talakad.

questions.q: Approximately how long did construction and embellishment of the Chennakeshava Temple complex reportedly take?
options: About a decade or several decades, spanning generations
questions.explanation: Work began in the 12th century and additions continued for decades under successive Hoysala patrons.

questions.q: What type of stone was primarily used for the carvings of the Chennakeshava Temple?
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples were carved from soft soapstone, which hardens with exposure and allows fine detail.

questions.q: The ornate perforated stone screens on the temple's walls are best described as which feature?
questions.explanation: The temple has many beautifully pierced stone screen windows in the Hoysala tradition.

questions.q: On what kind of platform do many Hoysala temples like Chennakeshava rest, allowing a circumambulatory path?
options: A raised star-shaped/stepped platform called a jagati
questions.explanation: The temple sits on a raised jagati platform that lets devotees circumambulate the shrine.

questions.q: Which famous group of sculpted female figures appears among the Chennakeshava Temple's bracket figures?
questions.explanation: The Madanika or salabhanjika bracket figures of celestial maidens are among Belur's most admired sculptures.

questions.q: A renowned standing pillar inside the temple's hall, celebrated as a masterwork, is commonly known as which?
questions.explanation: The lathe-turned Narasimha Pillar in the mantapa is famous for its dense, intricate carving.

questions.q: The Chennakeshava Temple at Belur is frequently paired with which nearby Hoysala monument?
questions.explanation: Belur's Chennakeshava is often visited together with the Hoysaleswara Temple at nearby Halebidu.

questions.q: Many of the Chennakeshava Temple's sculptures bear inscribed signatures of which group?
questions.explanation: Hoysala artisans often signed their work, and several sculptor names are inscribed at Belur.

questions.q: What is the tall, free-standing pillar set in the temple courtyard, often bearing a lamp or the emblem of Vishnu's mount, called?
questions.explanation: A free-standing pillar (stambha), such as the Garuda or lamp pillar, stands within the temple courtyard.

questions.q: The Chennakeshava Temple remains notable because it is which of the following today?
options: A ruined site with no worship
A still-active temple of living worship
questions.explanation: Unlike many ancient temples, Chennakeshava is still an active place of Hindu worship.

questions.q: The friezes around the base of the Chennakeshava Temple commonly depict which subjects?
options: Rows of elephants, lions, horses, and scenes from epics
questions.explanation: Horizontal bands at the base show elephants, lions, horsemen, and narrative scenes from the epics.

questions.q: Vishnuvardhana, the temple's patron, is associated with religious influence from which philosopher-saint?
questions.explanation: Vishnuvardhana is traditionally linked to the Vaishnava teacher Ramanuja, reflected in the temple's dedication to Vishnu.

questions.q: The principal sculptor often credited with key work at the Chennakeshava Temple was named which of the following?
questions.explanation: Legend and tradition credit the master sculptor Jakanachari with notable carvings at Belur.

questions.q: As of recent years, the Belur and Halebidu Hoysala temples have been associated with which UNESCO recognition?
options: Inscribed as part of the 'Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas' World Heritage Site
Never considered for any heritage status
Listed only as intangible cultural heritage
questions.explanation: In 2023 the Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas, including Belur, were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The temple's distinctive ground plan, with multiple projecting angles giving a many-pointed outline, is described as which shape?
questions.explanation: Hoysala shrines typically use a stellate, star-shaped plan that multiplies wall surfaces for carving.

questions.q: The decisive battle through which Vishnuvardhana's victory is often associated with the temple's founding was fought at which place?
questions.explanation: Vishnuvardhana's celebrated victory over the Cholas is associated with Talakad on the Kaveri.

questions.q: What is the original or alternate name by which the town of Belur was known in Hoysala-era records?
questions.explanation: Belur was known in inscriptions as Velapura, an early Hoysala capital.

questions.q: Which architectural tradition do Hoysala temples like Chennakeshava blend, distinguishing them from pure Dravidian or Nagara forms?
options: A Vesara synthesis drawing on Dravidian and Nagara elements
questions.explanation: Hoysala architecture is often classified within the Vesara mode, blending southern Dravidian and northern Nagara features.

questions.q: The main superstructure (tower over the sanctum) of the Chennakeshava Temple at Belur is notable today for which reason?
options: It is the tallest temple tower in India
Its original tall tower is missing/was not retained, leaving a relatively low profile
It is made entirely of gold
questions.explanation: The temple's original tall shikhara is no longer in place, giving the shrine a comparatively low silhouette today.
